title: Jasminanthes pilosa
uses: Jasminanthes pilosa, commonly known as the hairy jasmine, is used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant is traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and other secondary metabolites. In some Asian cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Jasminanthes pilosa for medicinal purposes.
taxonomy: Jasminanthes pilosa is classified within the family Loganiaceae and is further sorted under the order Gentianales. This plant is native to tropical Asia and is a member of the genus Jasminanthes. Jasminanthes pilosa is a flowering plant with various species, and its classification is closely related to other Loganiaceae family members. The plant's classification places it in the kingdom Plantae, and it is commonly found in moist, subtropical environments. Further research and classification may still be ongoing for this particular species within the Loganiaceae family.
benefits: Jasminanthes pilosa is a plant species that has been studied for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in wound healing and the prevention of infections. Additionally, some research suggests that Jasminanthes pilosa may have anti-cancer properties, although more studies are needed to confirm this. Further research is necessary to fully understand the potential health benefits of this plant.

It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. constituents: Jasminanthes pilosa contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are considered to be its active constituents. These compounds may contribute to the plant's potential therapeutic properties. Flavonoids in Jasminanthes pilosa may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. preparations: Jasminanthes pilosa is a plant used in traditional Chinese medicine, and its herbal preparations are believed to have various health benefits. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. intro: Jasminanthes pilosa, also known as 'Jin Yin Hua' in Chinese, is a plant species that belongs to the family Gentianaceae. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Jasminanthes pilosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Jasminanthes pilosa is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
